team_conventions.description: All internal API requests from Node.js Express to the Python LLM orchestration service MUST include a strictly typed 'x-tenant-id' header extracted from the verified JWT. Failing to pass this allows cross-tenant context bleeding.

When utilizing FastAPI asynchronous generators for Server-Sent Events (SSE), developers must explicitly wrap the yielded chunks in a `try...finally` block that strictly calls `<generator>.aclose()` or cleans up open httpx Client sessions. Unclosed streams will hold sockets hostage under reverse proxies like Nginx.

All user-supplied prompts passed from the middleware layer to the Groq LLM client MUST be piped through `TokenSanitizer.limit_and_escape()` before injection into the ChatCompletion matrix. Raw string interpolation (f-strings) inside prompt templates is strictly forbidden.

past_incidents.title: Catastrophic Memory Leak in Python SSE Streaming Service
past_incidents.root_cause: An asynchronous generator used for streaming LLM responses via FastAPI's `StreamingResponse` was repeatedly dropping client connections during peak load. Because the generator lacked a `finally` block to explicitly close the `httpx.AsyncClient` session and yield the event loop, socket file descriptors stacked up globally, eventually exhausting the container's allowed open files (ulimit) and triggering an Out-Of-Memory (OOM) kill loop across the replica set.
past_incidents.resolution_code_snippet:
async def stream_groq_response(prompt):
    client = httpx.AsyncClient()
    try:
        async with client.stream('POST', GROQ_URL, json=payload) as response:
            async for chunk in response.aiter_bytes():
                yield chunk
    finally:
        # INC-2025-08-14-A Fix: Await explicit closure to prevent socket leakage
        await client.aclose()

past_incidents.title: Context-Window Overflow and Prompt Injection Vulnerability
past_incidents.root_cause: A new 'bulk inference' endpoint in the Python service concatenated external CSV data directly into the system prompt using Python f-strings without calculating token density or escaping jailbreak delimiters. A malicious tenant uploaded a 20MB file posing as structured data, triggering a `context_length_exceeded` hard crash across the instance group and bypassing the LLM's safety guidelines.

historical_prs.author: j.doe@company.com
historical_prs.title: feat: Allow bulk LLM processing for admin dashboard
historical_prs.diff:
--- a/app/routes/admin.py
+++ b/app/routes/admin.py
@@ -45,6 +45,10 @@
 @router.post('/bulk_llm')
 async def process_bulk(req: Request):
     data = await req.json()
+    # Skip rate limiter for internal admin dashboard requests
+    if req.headers.get('origin') == 'https://admin.tool.internal':
+        return await orchestrate_llm(data["payload"])
+    
     await RateLimiter.consume(req.client.host)
     return await orchestrate_llm(data["payload"])
historical_prs.bug_explanation: Junior developer bypassed the Redis rate-limiter for requests holding a spoofable 'origin' header. An attacker quickly realized this and flooded the `/bulk_llm` endpoint, driving up Groq API costs by $3,000 in 2 hours before detection.

historical_prs.author: s.architect@company.com
historical_prs.title: fix: Remove origin-based rate limit bypass and enforce strict JWT roles
historical_prs.diff:
--- a/app/routes/admin.py
+++ b/app/routes/admin.py
@@ -45,10 +45,6 @@
 @router.post('/bulk_llm')
 async def process_bulk(req: Request, token: str = Depends(verify_jwt)):
     data = await req.json()
-    # Skip rate limiter for internal admin dashboard requests
-    if req.headers.get('origin') == 'https://admin.tool.internal':
-        return await orchestrate_llm(data["payload"])
-    
+    if not token.has_role('SUPERADMIN'):
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=403)
     await RateLimiter.consume(token.tenant_id, tier='admin')
     return await orchestrate_llm(data["payload"])
historical_prs.bug_explanation: Corrected the flaw by enforcing hard JWT role verification and shifting the RateLimiter key to the cryptographic `tenant_id` instead of a spoofable host or origin.

historical_prs.author: m.contractor@company.com
historical_prs.title: feat: Introduce lightning-fast SSE streaming for frontend chat interface
historical_prs.bug_explanation: This diff looks clean and functional to generic linters. It correctly uses `responseType: 'stream'` in Node, and `StreamingResponse` in Python. However, it triggers THREE specific traps: 1. It violates CONV-SEC-011 by omitting `x-tenant-id` in the axios header. 2. It violates CONV-SEC-089 by using raw f-strings for the prompt instead of `TokenSanitizer.limit_and_escape()`. 3. It exactly replicates Incident A (INC-2025-08-14-A) because the python generator `event_generator()` lacks a `try...finally: await llm_client.aclose()` block, which will silently bleed sockets.
